marketState: neutral-to-bearish — fairly valued spot pressured by a storage surplus and a bearish daily/weekly trend, partly offset by a near-term heat demand spike
summary: Front price ($2.888) sits almost exactly at model fair value ($2.917, band $2.417-$3.417), so there is no large mispricing to exploit outright. The bigger tell is structural: storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average and this week's build beat forecast by 72 Bcf (bearish surprise), while price is already running -15.2% YTD versus a -3.4% median year (-11.8pp vsMedian), though the seasonality signal itself carries low reliability (50pp 10-90 spread) and 4-week up-probability is only 31%. Daily and weekly technicals show a bearish trend (50<200 EMA, negative MACD), with 4h/1d RSI oversold (25.2/33.6) and a 1d close below the lower Bollinger band flagging a stretched move that could bounce before continuing. The clear counterweight is near-term weather: 1-7 day demand is 25.7% above the 5-year norm and above the top of its historical range, which should support spot prices short-term even as the 8-16 day reading cools back to a near-normal +8.2%. Managed money is net short but only at the 35th percentile of its 5-year range, so positioning is not crowded enough to expect a violent squeeze either way.

longTerm.entry: $2.90 - $3.00 (sell into a bounce toward fair value)
longTerm.rationale: Storage sits well above the 5-year average with a bearish weekly surprise (+72 Bcf vs -11 Bcf forecast), and price is already depressed versus its seasonal median with a negative typical 4-week move (-3.1%) and only 31% odds of being higher in 4 weeks. COT is not crowded (35th percentile net-short), leaving room for further short build without an imminent squeeze, and the futures curve's steep winter carry (Dec/Jan roughly +$0.9-1.4 vs front) is normal seasonal structure rather than a bullish distortion.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained daily close above ~$3.20 (through the Nov-contract level and upper fair-value drift), or a string of storage draws/undershoots versus forecast that erase the surplus, would invalidate the short thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Price sits almost exactly at model fair value ($2.888 vs $2.917, status FAIR) so there is no clean mispricing edge to trade. Extreme near-term heat demand (+25.7% vs 5y, above the historical range max) is fighting a confirmed daily/weekly downtrend with oversold RSI on 4h/1d (25.2/33.6) — a genuine two-way setup rather than a tradeable signal right now.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a daily close above ~$3.00 confirming a bullish reversal off oversold conditions — then go long toward $3.10-3.15 — or (b) a rejection near the $2.95-3.00 fair-value zone with 1h/4h RSI rolling back over — then go short toward $2.70-2.75 near the lower daily Bollinger band.
